subject: The investment case behind the food
preheader: FarmThru is opening to investors on Birchal. Here is what you need to know.
body:
Hi {first_name},

Since you signed up, you have seen the food and met the farms. Here is the question: what if you could own a piece of this?

FarmThru is opening an equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal in mid-2026. For the first time, you can own a share of the grocery store you buy from.

Here is how the business works. Regenerative farms across NSW send their products to our Brookvale hub. Families order online and collect Monday to Friday. Zero wholesalers. Zero cold-storage warehousing. The food is days old, not weeks. The farmer earns a fairer price.

The Australian grocery market is estimated at $130 billion. Direct-to-consumer food is one of its fastest growing segments. Families increasingly want to know the farm behind every product. That is exactly what FarmThru provides.

The terms: minimum investment $50. Maximum $10,000 per investor. Full details, financials, and risk disclosure will be in the offer document on Birchal.

We want to be upfront. Investing in early-stage businesses carries real risk. You could lose some or all of your investment. We will not downplay that.

If you want first access before the campaign opens to everyone, a $5 refundable deposit secures your VIP spot. That means priority access, founder updates, and an exclusive Q&A.

Consider seeking independent financial advice. The disclosure document will be available on Birchal when the campaign launches.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
creative_brief: Investment email. Clean, data-forward design. Key business model illustrated simply (farm to hub to family). VIP deposit CTA button below. Birchal badge in footer. Honest, confident tone. No hype.
primary_text: Since you signed up, you have seen the food and met the farms. Here is the question: what if you could own a piece of this?
headline: The investment case behind the food
